content:
A full adder and an XOR gate are used to design a digital circuit with inputs $X, Y$, and $Z$, and output $F$, as shown below. The input $Z$ is connected to the carry-in input of the full adder.

If the input $Z$ is set to logic ' 1 ', then the circuit functions as __________ with $X$ and $Y$ as inputs.

options.content: a binary to Gray code converter

explanation:
Given : Full adder and XOR gate are used to design a digital circuits with input $X, Y$ and $Z$ and output $F$ of shown below

When $Z$ is set to 1 , the inputs to full adder are $X$ and $\bar{Y}$ and input carry is 1 then output $F$ is

$$ \begin{aligned} & F=X+\bar{Y}+1 \\ & F=X+2 \text { ' s complement of } Y \\ & F=X-Y \end{aligned} $$

Hence the given circuit functions as subtractor with inputs $X$ and $Y$ when $Z=1$

Hence, the correct option is (B).

content: In the circuit shown below, P and Q are the inputs. The logical function realized by the circuit shown below is
explanation:
The output of MUX is given as, $Y=\bar{S} I_0+S I_1$

Given : $S=Q, I_0=0, I_1=P$

$$ \begin{aligned} & Y=\bar{Q} \cdot 0+Q \cdot P \\\\ & Y=P Q \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (A).

content: Consider the 2-bit multiplexer (MUX) shown in the figure. For OUTPUT to be the XOR of C and D, the values for A0, A1, A2 and A3 are ___________.
explanation:
The output of MUX, F is

$$F = {\overline S _1}{\overline S _0}{I_0} + {\overline S _1}{S_0}{I_1} + {S_1}{\overline S _0}{I_2} + {S_1}{S_0}{I_3}$$

$$F = \overline C \overline D {A_0} + \overline C D{A_1} + C\overline D {A_2} + CD{A_3}$$

But output, $$F = C\, \oplus \,D = \overline C D + C\overline D $$

$$\therefore$$ Inputs of MUX are, A0 = 0, A1 = 1, A2 = 1, A3 = 0

content:
The propagation delays of the XOR gate, AND gate and multiplexer (MUX) in the circuit shown in the figure are $4 \mathrm{~ns}, 2 \mathrm{~ns}$ and 1 ns respectively.

If all the inputs $P, Q, R, S$ and $T$ are applied simultaneously and held constant, the maximum propagation delay of the circuit is
explanation:
Given :

Delay of XOR gate $=4 \mathrm{~ns}$

Delay of AND gate $=2 \mathrm{~ns}$

Delay of MUX=1ns

Case 1 :

Assuming $T=0$ then selection line of MUX $S_0=0$, so that MUX input ' 0 ' get enable so path followed by signal in the given circuit is shown by dotted lines as,

So total propagation delay $\tau_1$ from input to output is,

$$ \begin{aligned} & \tau_1=(\text { Propagation delay of AND gate })+(\text { Propagation delay of MUX- } 2) \\ & \tau_1=2 n s+1 n s=3 n s \end{aligned} $$

Hence MUX input ' 0 ' get enable then propagation delay of given circuit $\tau_1=3 n s$

Case 2 :

Assuming $T=1$ then selection line of MUX is $S_0=1$, so that MUX input ' 1 ' get enable so path followed by signal in the given circuit is shown by dotted lines as,

So total propagation delay $\tau_2$ from input to output is,

$\tau_2=($ Propagation delay of AND gate $)+($ Propagation delay of MUX-1 $)+$ (Propagation delay of AND gate) + (Propagation delay of MUX-2)

$\tau_2=2 n s+1 n s+2 n s+1 n s=6 n s$

Hence MUX input ' 1 ' get enable then propagation delay of given circuit $\tau_2=6 n s$

Hence maximum delay of circuit is $\operatorname{MAX}\left(\tau_1, \tau_2\right)=\operatorname{MAX}(3 \mathrm{~ns}, 6 \mathrm{~ns})=6 \mathrm{~ns}$

Hence, the correct option is (C).

content: The figure below shows a multiplexer where $S_1$ and $S_0$ are the select lines, $I_0$ to $I_3$ are the input data lines, $E N$ is the enable line, and $F(P, Q, R)$ is the output. $F$ is
explanation:
$$ \text { Given } 4 \times 1 \text { multiplexer is shown in figure, } $$

The output function will be given as,

$$ \begin{aligned} & F=\bar{P} \bar{Q} \cdot R+\bar{P} Q \cdot 0+P \bar{Q} \cdot R+P Q \cdot 1 \\ & F=\bar{P} \bar{Q} \cdot R+P \bar{Q} \cdot R+P Q \cdot 1 \\ & F=\bar{Q} R(\bar{P}+P)+P Q \\ & F=\bar{Q} R+P Q \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (D).

The minimum function that can detect a "divisible by 3" 8421  BCD code digit  (representation is D<sub>8</sub> D<sub>4</sub> D<sub>2</sub> D<sub>1</sub>) is given by:

A 4-bit priority encoder has inputs $D_3, D_2, D_1,$ and $D_0$ in descending order of priority. The two-bit output $AB$ is generated as 00, 01, 10, and 11 corresponding to inputs $D_3, D_2, D_1,$ and $D_0$, respectively. The Boolean expression of the output bit $B$ is _______.
explanation:
Given : 4-bit priority encodes

Inputs $D_3, D_2, D_1, D_0$ are in descending order of priority.

The two bits output A B is generated as 00,01 , 10 and 11 corresponding to inputs $D_3, D_2, D_1$ and $D_0$.

The truth table of priority encodes is

$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|cc|} \hline D_3 & D_2 & D_1 & D_0 & \mathrm{~A} & \mathrm{~B} \\ \hline 1 & \times & \times & \times & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & \times & \times & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 & \times & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 1 & 1 \\ \hline 0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & \times & \times \\ \hline \end{array} $$

$$ \mathrm{K} \text { Map for output } B \text { is, } $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &B=\bar{D}_3 D_2+\bar{D}_3 \bar{D}_1\\ &\text { Hence, the correct option is (B). } \end{aligned} $$

content:
The propagation delay of the 2 x 1 MUX shown in the circuit is 10 ns. Consider the propagation delay of the inverter as 0 ns.

If S is set to 1 then the output Y is _______.
explanation:
$$ \text { Given : } 2 \times 1 \text { MUX circuit is shown below } $$

Propogation delay of $2 \times 1$ MUX circuit $=10 \mathrm{nS}$

Propogation delay of invester $=0 \mathrm{nS}$

For $S=1$, MUX circuit is shown below

When $S=1$, input data line ' 1 ' is selected and MUX circuit can be reduced to a inverter with feedback an shown below

Hence, output $y$ is a square waveform an shown below

Time period of output square wave is

$$ \begin{aligned} & T=10 \mathrm{~ns}+10 \mathrm{~ns} \\ & T=20 \mathrm{~ns} \end{aligned} $$

Frequency of output square wave is

$$ \begin{aligned} & f=\frac{1}{T} \\ & f=\frac{1}{20 n s}=\frac{1}{20} \times 10^9 \\ & f=\frac{1000}{20} \times 10^6=50 \mathrm{MHz} \end{aligned} $$

So, the output is a square wave of frequency 50 MHz .

Hence, the correct option is (B).

content:
A four-variable Boolean function is realized using 4 $$ \times $$ 1 multiplexers as shown in the figure.
The minimized expression for F(U, V, W, X) is

options.content: $$\left( {UV + \overline U \overline V } \right)\left( {\overline W \overline X  + \overline W X} \right)$$
$$\left( {U\overline V  + \overline U V} \right)\left( {\overline W \overline X  + \overline W X} \right)$$

explanation:
We know

Output expression of 4 $$ \times $$ 1 MUX is,

F = $${\overline S _1}{\overline S _0}{I_0}$$ + $${\overline S _1}{S_0}{I_1}$$ + $${S_1}{\overline S _0}{I_2} + {S_1}{S_2}{I_3}$$

For first MUX,

f1(U, V) = $$\overline U \overline V .0 + \overline U V.1 + U\overline V .1 + UV.0$$

f1(U, V) = $$\overline U V + U\overline V$$

For second MUX,

F(U, V, W, X) = $${\overline W \overline X }$$.f1 + $${\overline W X}$$.f1 + $${W\overline X }$$.0 + $$WX.0$$

F(U, V, W, X) = $${\overline W {f_1}\left( {X + \overline X } \right)}$$

F(U, V, W, X) = $${\overline W {f_1}}$$ = $$\overline W \left( {U\overline V + \overline U V} \right)$$

content:
Signals A,B,C,D and $$\overline D $$ are available. Using a single 8 - to - 1 multiplexer and no other gate, implement the Boolean function.

$$f(A,B,C,D) = B.C + A.B.\bar D + \bar A.\bar C.\bar D$$

A chemical reactor has three sensors indicating the following conditions:-
(1) Pressure (P) is low or high'
(2) Temperature (T) is low or high' and
(3) Liquid level (L) is low or high.

its has two controls - Heater (H) which is either on or off and inlet value (V) which is open or close. The controls are operated as per Table.

(a) Using the convertion High =1, Low = 0, On=1, Off=0, Open=1 and Closed=0, draw the Karnaugh maps for H and V.

(b) Obtain the minimal product of sums expressions for H and V.

(c) Realize the logic for H and V using two 4-input multiplexers with T and L as control inputs. Used T as MSB.
